gfm-tokenization.claim_text: Tokenization strategy materially affects what genomic foundation models can learn — k-mer, BPE, and single-nucleotide schemes induce distinct biases on context length, motif resolution, and downstream transfer.

candidates.paper_title: DNABERT: pre-trained Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers model for DNA-language in genome

useful_outcomes.rationale: DNABERT uses overlapping k-mer tokenization and demonstrates SOTA gains on promoter, splice site, and TF-binding prediction over prior methods.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Pretrained DNABERT and code are released; researchers can adopt the k-mer tokenizer scheme today via the published Bioinformatics tooling.

plausible.rationale: Methodology of k-mer BERT pretraining with downstream fine-tuning is sound and supported by extensive cross-task benchmarks and motif visualizations.

scalable.rationale: K-mer tokenization plus quadratic attention scales modestly; cited 512-token windows limit genome-scale use beyond local regulatory regions.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validates via fine-tuning on promoter/splice/TF-binding tasks, motif extraction, and cross-organism transfer, providing a borrowable tokenizer-comparison protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Nucleotide Transformer: building and evaluating robust foundation models for human genomics

useful_outcomes.rationale: Nucleotide Transformer compares 6-mer and BPE-style tokenization across model scales (50M-2.5B), showing tokenizer choice impacts genomic phenotype prediction.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Pretrained NT models, tokenizers, and benchmarks are released on HuggingFace, immediately usable in multimodal genomic pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Methodology grounded in 3,202 human + 850 species genomes with low-data fine-tuning evidence supporting transfer-quality claims.

scalable.rationale: Up to 2.5B parameters but context limited to ~6-12kb tokens; tokenizer enables longer effective context yet not full-genome scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides 18-task benchmark suite spanning regulatory and splice tasks plus variant prioritization — a directly borrowable validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: The Nucleotide Transformer: Building and Evaluating Robust Foundation Models for Human Genomics

useful_outcomes.rationale: Preprint version of Nucleotide Transformer demonstrates tokenizer/scale effects on molecular phenotype prediction across diverse human and species genomes.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Code and weights released; preprint slightly less polished than Nature Methods version but readily adoptable.

plausible.rationale: Same methodology as published version: rigorous multi-genome pretraining and downstream fine-tuning evidence.

scalable.rationale: Same scale and context limits as the published NT; tokenizer choice constrains genome-scale modeling.

how_to_validate.rationale: Same multi-task benchmark and variant-prioritization validation strategy as published version.

candidates.paper_title: HyenaDNA: Long-Range Genomic Sequence Modeling at Single Nucleotide Resolution

useful_outcomes.rationale: HyenaDNA explicitly contrasts single-nucleotide tokenization vs k-mer/BPE, showing single-nt resolution matters for SNP-level effects with measured downstream gains.

immediate_benefit.rationale: HyenaDNA is open-sourced with a 1M-token single-nt tokenizer; immediately adoptable for genomic foundation pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Direct demonstration: Hyena's implicit-convolution backbone enables single-nt tokenization; ablations on token granularity strongly support the claim.

scalable.rationale: Sub-quadratic in length up to 1M tokens; 500x context increase makes single-nt tokenization scalable to chromosome-scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validates with Nucleotide Transformer benchmark and species-classification long-context tasks; cross-token-scheme comparisons support the validation strategy.

candidates.paper_title: Multi-purpose RNA language modelling with motif-aware pretraining and type-guided fine-tuning

useful_outcomes.rationale: RNAErnie introduces motif-level masking on top of base/subsequence tokenization, with measured gains (1.8-3.3%) tied to tokenization-aware pretraining choices.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Method is published with clear architecture; some engineering required to integrate motif-tokenization into existing GFM stacks.

plausible.rationale: Solid evaluation on 7 datasets/5 tasks supporting the tokenization+motif strategy with consistent gains.

scalable.rationale: Transformer backbone limits context; tokenization scheme adds motif vocab but base scale is moderate.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides RNA-specific multi-task benchmark and ablations on motif tokenization that a multimodal team could borrow.

candidates.paper_title: Genomic language model predicts protein co-regulation and function

useful_outcomes.rationale: gLM uses protein-token-level inputs in a metagenomic context; tokenization is discussed but is not the central claim of the paper.

immediate_benefit.rationale: gLM model and code are accessible; tokenization choice is implicit (protein embeddings) rather than a focus.

plausible.rationale: Sound methodology for gene-context modeling but tokenization comparisons are not provided.

scalable.rationale: Metagenomic scaffold scale is moderate; tokenization at protein level reduces sequence length substantially.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validation via attention/operon analysis; not a tokenization-comparison study.

candidates.paper_title: Sequence modeling and design from molecular to genome scale with Evo

useful_outcomes.rationale: Evo uses byte-level single-nucleotide tokenization at 131kb context, demonstrating tokenizer choice matters for whole-genome generation tasks.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Evo weights and code are released, including the byte tokenizer for direct adoption.

plausible.rationale: 7B-parameter model trained on prokaryotic genomes with strong zero-shot results supports byte-tokenization claim.

scalable.rationale: Single-nt byte tokenization scales to 131kb context and 650kb generation, suitable for genome-scale multimodal use.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides zero-shot fitness, gene essentiality, and CRISPR-Cas generation tasks as borrowable validation.

candidates.paper_title: The EN-TEx resource of multi-tissue personal epigenomes & variant-impact models

useful_outcomes.rationale: EN-TEx is primarily an epigenome resource; tokenization is not discussed as a core variable.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Resource is publicly available, but it doesn't provide tokenizer guidance for GFMs.

plausible.rationale: Deep-learning model is mentioned but tokenization details are tangential to the paper's contribution.

scalable.rationale: Per-tissue datasets and models scale moderately; not a tokenizer scaling study.

how_to_validate.rationale: Focus is on allele-specific activity prediction validation, not tokenization choice.

candidates.paper_title: Self-supervised learning on millions of primary RNA sequences from 72 vertebrates improves sequence-based RNA splicing prediction

useful_outcomes.rationale: SpliceBERT uses base-level tokenization; the paper compares per-base vs k-mer choices implicitly through architecture but tokenization is not central.

immediate_benefit.rationale: SpliceBERT is released; pretraining-on-72-species recipe is reproducible with standard tokenizers.

plausible.rationale: Sound method for splice prediction; tokenization choice is justified but not extensively ablated.

scalable.rationale: Transformer backbone with limited context; multi-species data scales but per-token granularity limits range.

how_to_validate.rationale: Cross-species splice-site validation provides a borrowable evaluation, partly relevant to tokenization questions.

candidates.paper_title: GENA-LM: a family of open-source foundational DNA language models for long sequences

useful_outcomes.rationale: GENA-LM explicitly compares BPE and other tokenization schemes for long-context DNA modeling, with downstream task gains.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open-source models with multiple tokenizers released on HuggingFace, immediately usable.

plausible.rationale: Methodology supports BPE+recurrent memory for handling 36kb inputs across taxon-specific models.

scalable.rationale: Recurrent memory mechanism extends sequences beyond 36kb at modest computational cost.

how_to_validate.rationale: Variety of downstream tasks demonstrated; tokenizer ablation is partial rather than exhaustive.

gfm-long-context.claim_text: Long-context architectures (state-space models, Hyena, Mamba, sliding-window attention) unlock genome-scale sequence modeling well beyond traditional Transformer context limits, enabling distal regulatory interactions to be learned end-to-end.

candidates.paper_title: Effective gene expression prediction from sequence by integrating long-range interactions

useful_outcomes.rationale: Enformer integrates long-range interactions up to 100kb via dilated/sliding attention, with measured gains in expression prediction over short-context CNNs.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Enformer is widely available with code and weights; readily adopted as a long-context genomic backbone today.

plausible.rationale: Strong methodology with extensive expression and enhancer-promoter benchmarks supporting the long-range architectural claim.

scalable.rationale: Reaches 100kb context but not full-genome; widely used in production multimodal pipelines.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validated on natural variants, MPRA saturation mutagenesis, and enhancer-promoter calls — strong borrowable validation suite.

candidates.paper_title: Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces

useful_outcomes.rationale: Mamba is a general selective-SSM architecture demonstrating linear-time long-context modeling, with explicit genomics benchmarks showing strong performance.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Mamba code is open-source; integrating into genomic stacks is straightforward and underlies subsequent GFMs (Caduceus, Evo).

plausible.rationale: Rigorous architectural derivation with hardware-aware parallel algorithm and SOTA results across modalities including genomics.

scalable.rationale: Linear scaling to million-length sequences with 5x throughput vs Transformers; ideal for genome-scale contexts.

how_to_validate.rationale: Genomics validation is part of broader benchmarks; not a genome-specific validation suite.

candidates.paper_title: CADD v1.7: using protein language models, regulatory CNNs and other nucleotide-level scores to improve genome-wide variant predictions

useful_outcomes.rationale: CADD v1.7 incorporates regulatory CNN and ESM scores, but does not advance long-context architectures themselves.

immediate_benefit.rationale: CADD service and scores are publicly accessible but not a long-context architecture per se.

plausible.rationale: Methodology is sound but combines existing scores rather than establishing new long-context modeling.

scalable.rationale: CADD scoring is genome-wide but underlying CNN/protein-LM components are not long-context.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validation via ClinVar/gnomAD/MPRA is strong but does not isolate long-context contributions.

candidates.paper_title: Nucleotide Transformer: building and evaluating robust foundation models for human genomics

useful_outcomes.rationale: Nucleotide Transformer extends context modestly via tokenization but uses standard attention; long-context is not its primary innovation.

immediate_benefit.rationale: NT models are immediately usable; effective context up to ~6-12kb fine-tuned via standard transformer infra.

plausible.rationale: Solid methodology but the long-context claim is moderate — relies on tokenizer compression, not an architectural advance.

scalable.rationale: Scale to 2.5B params helps but quadratic attention still limits genome-scale context.

how_to_validate.rationale: Multi-task benchmark validates long-range gene-expression and chromatin tasks at fine-tuning context.

candidates.paper_title: Cell-type-directed design of synthetic enhancers

useful_outcomes.rationale: Synthetic enhancer design uses CNN-based deep models rather than long-context architectures.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Method is reproducible for enhancer design but doesn't supply a long-context architecture for adoption.

plausible.rationale: Sound experimental work but tangential to the long-context architectural claim.

scalable.rationale: Operates on short enhancer sequences (<2kb); not genome-scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Strong transgenic validation but specific to enhancer design, not long-context modeling.

candidates.paper_title: HyenaDNA: Long-Range Genomic Sequence Modeling at Single Nucleotide Resolution

useful_outcomes.rationale: HyenaDNA explicitly demonstrates 1M-token single-nt context with sub-quadratic Hyena operator, directly proving long-context architectures unlock genome-scale modeling.

immediate_benefit.rationale: HyenaDNA code and pretrained weights are open and broadly used as a long-context GFM backbone today.

plausible.rationale: Empirical results on long-context benchmarks (chromatin profile, species classification) directly support the architectural claim.

scalable.rationale: Sub-quadratic scaling, 160x faster training, full global context at single-nt resolution to 1M tokens.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validates on Nucleotide Transformer benchmark + species-ID long-context probe; clear borrowable validation strategy.

candidates.paper_title: Genomic language model predicts protein co-regulation and function

useful_outcomes.rationale: gLM operates on gene-token-level metagenomic scaffolds, achieving long contexts measured in genes rather than nucleotides.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Code is available; extending gLM to nucleotide-level long-context requires additional engineering.

plausible.rationale: Methodology is sound for protein-level operon learning but not a nucleotide long-context architecture.

scalable.rationale: Gene-token compression yields long effective contexts but not at single-nucleotide resolution.

how_to_validate.rationale: Operon and attention-pattern validation; partial relevance to long-context claim.

candidates.paper_title: Sequence modeling and design from molecular to genome scale with Evo

useful_outcomes.rationale: Evo achieves 131kb single-nucleotide context using StripedHyena, generating coding sequences up to 650kb — strong direct support for long-context architectures.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Evo weights and code are released; substantial compute needed but architecture is reusable.

plausible.rationale: 7B-parameter model with strong zero-shot performance on whole-genome tasks supports long-context architectural claim.

scalable.rationale: Scales to 131kb context at byte resolution and generates 650kb sequences — production-suitable for genome-scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Zero-shot fitness, gene essentiality, and CRISPR-Cas generation provide borrowable long-context validation tasks.

candidates.paper_title: Species-aware DNA language models capture regulatory elements and their evolution

useful_outcomes.rationale: Species-aware DNA LM captures regulatory elements across 800 species using transformer with moderate context.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Species-aware DNA-LM weights are accessible; long-context is not the primary architectural focus.

plausible.rationale: Sound methodology focused on conservation, not on long-context architecture per se.

scalable.rationale: Standard transformer backbone — long-context is moderate.

how_to_validate.rationale: MPRA, motif, and gene-expression validations provide partial coverage of long-context claim.

candidates.paper_title: Progress and opportunities of foundation models in bioinformatics

useful_outcomes.rationale: Survey on FMs in bioinformatics covers long-context architectures conceptually rather than introducing new evidence.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Useful orientation document for choosing long-context FM approaches; no new artifacts.

plausible.rationale: Well-structured review; doesn't advance methodology directly.

scalable.rationale: Discusses scalability of long-context approaches at a high level.

how_to_validate.rationale: General validation guidance only; not a concrete reusable protocol.

gfm-pretraining-objective.claim_text: Self-supervised pretraining objectives — masked language modeling, autoregressive generation, span corruption, contrastive learning — determine downstream transfer quality and the kinds of biological structure (motifs, conservation, splicing, 3D contacts) the model captures.

candidates.paper_title: scGPT: toward building a foundation model for single-cell multi-omics using generative AI

useful_outcomes.rationale: scGPT introduces a generative (autoregressive-style) pretraining objective tailored to single-cell data with strong transfer to multiple downstream tasks.

immediate_benefit.rationale: scGPT code and weights are widely available, providing a directly adoptable generative pretraining recipe.

plausible.rationale: Methodology over 33M cells with consistent SOTA gains supports the objective-choice claim.

scalable.rationale: 100M+ params and 33M-cell training scales appropriately for single-cell foundation models.

how_to_validate.rationale: Cell-type annotation, batch integration, perturbation prediction tasks form a strong borrowable validation suite.

candidates.paper_title: Evaluating Protein Transfer Learning with TAPE

useful_outcomes.rationale: TAPE benchmarks multiple SSL objectives (MLM, LM, contrastive) on protein tasks, directly demonstrating objective choice impacts transfer quality.

immediate_benefit.rationale: TAPE benchmark and code are public; objective-comparison framework is directly reusable.

plausible.rationale: Rigorous comparison with controlled baselines supports the claim that pretraining objectives determine transfer.

scalable.rationale: Protein-domain results may not directly translate to genome-scale DNA/RNA contexts.

how_to_validate.rationale: Five biologically relevant tasks with held-out splits provide an exemplary validation strategy.

candidates.paper_title: A foundation model for clinical-grade computational pathology and rare cancers detection

useful_outcomes.rationale: Virchow is a histopathology image foundation model; pretraining objective claims do not transfer to DNA/RNA GFMs.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Image-domain artifacts not adoptable for genomic multimodal stacks.

plausible.rationale: Methodology is sound for pathology but tangential to GFM SSL objectives.

scalable.rationale: Scales for pathology; not relevant to genome-scale sequence modeling.

how_to_validate.rationale: Pathology benchmarks not borrowable for genomic SSL objective evaluation.

candidates.paper_title: The Nucleotide Transformer: Building and Evaluating Robust Foundation Models for Human Genomics

useful_outcomes.rationale: Nucleotide Transformer ablates MLM masking strategies and scales across model sizes, demonstrating objective+scale effects on transfer.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Pretrained NT recipe is reproducible; MLM-on-DNA configuration is directly adoptable.

plausible.rationale: Comprehensive ablations on objective and scale support the SSL-objective claim.

scalable.rationale: MLM-based; scale up to 2.5B params; quadratic attention limits but objective is well-tested.

how_to_validate.rationale: 18-task benchmark plus variant prioritization gives a strong borrowable SSL evaluation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: Interpretable RNA Foundation Model from Unannotated Data for Highly Accurate RNA Structure and Function Predictions

useful_outcomes.rationale: RNA-FM uses MLM SSL on 23M ncRNA sequences; demonstrates downstream gains across structure, regulation, and SARS-CoV-2 prediction.

immediate_benefit.rationale: RNA-FM weights and code are available; MLM recipe directly adoptable for RNA tasks.

plausible.rationale: Solid SSL methodology with broad downstream evidence supporting the objective claim.

scalable.rationale: Transformer backbone limits context but RNA scale (23M) is substantial.

how_to_validate.rationale: Multi-task downstream validation including 3D structure and protein-RNA binding.

candidates.paper_title: Accurate RNA 3D structure prediction using a language model-based deep learning approach

useful_outcomes.rationale: RhoFold+ leverages an RNA LM pretrained on 23.7M sequences via SSL for downstream 3D structure prediction.

immediate_benefit.rationale: RhoFold+ pipeline and pretrained LM are released; adoptable for RNA structural pipelines.

plausible.rationale: Strong empirical results on RNA-Puzzles and CASP15 support the SSL-pretraining benefit.

scalable.rationale: Transformer+structure module; scale is moderate, not genome-scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Cross-family, cross-type, and time-censored validations provide a borrowable evaluation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: scGPT: Towards Building a Foundation Model for Single-Cell Multi-omics Using Generative AI

useful_outcomes.rationale: scGPT preprint version with same generative SSL objective on single-cell data; identical objective-driven gains.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Released code and weights; preprint is functionally equivalent to Nature Methods version.

plausible.rationale: Same methodology with 33M-cell pretraining and downstream evaluation.

scalable.rationale: Same scale characteristics as published version.

how_to_validate.rationale: Same comprehensive downstream evaluation suite.

candidates.paper_title: HyenaDNA: Long-Range Genomic Sequence Modeling at Single Nucleotide Resolution

useful_outcomes.rationale: HyenaDNA uses next-token autoregressive pretraining at single-nt resolution, showing AR objective scales with long context for downstream gains.

immediate_benefit.rationale: HyenaDNA pretraining recipe (AR + Hyena) is open and reusable.

plausible.rationale: Empirical evidence supports AR objective at single-nt resolution as effective for transfer.

scalable.rationale: AR objective combined with Hyena scales to 1M tokens — production-suitable for genome-scale SSL.

how_to_validate.rationale: Long-context probes and standard genomic benchmarks validate the AR-pretraining choice.

candidates.paper_title: Universal Cell Embeddings: A Foundation Model for Cell Biology

useful_outcomes.rationale: UCE uses self-supervised cross-species cell embedding without explicit objective comparison; demonstrates SSL value on cell biology.

immediate_benefit.rationale: UCE artifacts are public; SSL pipeline reproducible but objective ablations limited.

plausible.rationale: Methodology is sound but objective comparison is not the central focus.

scalable.rationale: Scales to 36M cells across 8 species; embedding space transfers without fine-tuning.

how_to_validate.rationale: Emergent-behavior probes provide partial borrowable validation.

candidates.paper_title: Large Scale Foundation Model on Single-cell Transcriptomics

useful_outcomes.rationale: scFoundation 100M-parameter SSL on 50M cells demonstrates pretraining-objective benefit for single-cell downstream tasks.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Model is released; SSL training recipe reproducible at moderate cost.

plausible.rationale: Methodology supports the objective claim but lacks fine-grained objective ablations.

scalable.rationale: 100M parameters and 50M-cell training scales well for single-cell SSL.

how_to_validate.rationale: Multiple downstream tasks (drug response, perturbation) provide partial validation strategy.
